meta.title: iOS App Development Cost: iPhone App Estimate
meta.description: Get a clear iPhone app estimate in Swift/SwiftUI or cross-platform, with TestFlight builds, App Store Connect setup, and App Review readiness.

hero.h1: ios app development cost: get a clear estimate for your iPhone app
hero.subtitle: Plan scope, validate with TestFlight, and ship through App Store Connect with confidence.

pain.title: Why iPhone apps go over budget
bullets: Have you ever tried pricing an app before scope was locked and paid twice for rework
Have you ever gotten stuck on certificates, provisioning, and App Store Connect access when you needed a build
Have you ever cleared QA, then lost days in App Review due to HIG, privacy prompts, or Sign in with Apple

benefits.title: Scope you can trust
benefits.desc: You get a proposal with clear deliverables, acceptance criteria, and risk flags so estimates stay stable as you build.

benefits.title: Apple-ready delivery
benefits.desc: You ship with Human Interface Guidelines alignment, privacy-friendly permissions, accessibility basics, and App Store review considerations.

benefits.title: TestFlight to release
benefits.desc: You receive signed builds, TestFlight distribution, and an App Store Connect submission checklist that reduces last-minute surprises.

scope.title: What’s included in iOS development
bullets: App Store Connect setup and bundle ID
Native Swift with SwiftUI or UIKit
Sign in with Apple authentication flow
Human Interface Guidelines and UX checks

useCases.title: MVP launch
useCases.desc: You ship the essential iPhone flows fast, then iterate using real user feedback.

useCases.title: Subscription app
useCases.desc: You implement In-App Purchases, paywalls, restore flows, and App Store rules safely.

useCases.title: Secure login
useCases.desc: You add Sign in with Apple and session handling that fits common iOS privacy expectations.

useCases.title: Push-first product
useCases.desc: You deliver APNs notifications with opt-in messaging, preferences, and event-based triggers.

useCases.title: iOS rebuild
useCases.desc: You modernize an existing product with SwiftUI priorities and consistent iPhone UX.

options.name: Swift + SwiftUI
options.note: Great for modern UI, smooth performance, and strong alignment with Apple patterns.

options.name: Swift + UIKit
options.note: A solid choice for complex screens, legacy components, and mature iOS integrations.

options.name: Flutter
options.note: Cross-platform speed with room for iOS-native work like APNs, IAP, and signing.

options.name: React Native
options.note: Cross-platform delivery with an ecosystem that often needs careful native module work.

options.name: iOS CI/CD
options.note: Automated builds, signing, and releases for TestFlight and App Store Connect workflows.

steps.title: Align on scope
steps.desc: You share screens, roles, integrations, and constraints so scope stays explicit.

steps.title: Estimate by milestones
steps.desc: You receive a staged estimate with deliverables, risks, and acceptance checkpoints.

steps.title: Build and publish
steps.desc: You validate via TestFlight, then submit through App Store Connect with guidance.

faq.q: What drives iOS app development cost the most?
faq.a: Scope is the biggest driver: number of screens, backend integrations, Sign in with Apple, APNs, In-App Purchases, and QA plus App Review prep.

faq.q: Will you publish the app in my Apple account?
faq.a: Yes. Your app should live in your App Store Connect account, and you remain the owner while we guide access, signing, and submission.

faq.q: Can I test builds before App Store submission?
faq.a: Yes. We use TestFlight so you and testers can review builds, report issues, and approve fixes before final review.

faq.q: Do I need Sign in with Apple for my app?
faq.a: It depends on your login options. If you offer third-party sign-in, Apple may require Sign in with Apple too, so we confirm it early.

faq.q: Should you choose native or Flutter/React Native?
faq.a: Native Swift/SwiftUI is often the safest for iOS polish and performance. Flutter or React Native can reduce time when you also target Android, depending on integrations.

ctaFinal.title: Get a realistic iPhone app estimate
ctaFinal.desc: Tell us what you want to ship and you get scope, milestones, timeline, and a clear budget.
